title: Ansible Performance Tuning
learningObjectives: Configure SSH multiplexing and pipelining for faster execution
Use free and host_pinned strategies for parallel execution
Implement async tasks and polling for long-running operations
Profile playbooks with callback plugins and optimize bottlenecks

theory.content:
SSH Performance

By default, Ansible opens a new SSH connection for each task. Enable these settings in ansible.cfg to dramatically improve speed:

[ssh_connection]
pipelining = True          # Reduces SSH connections per task
control_path_dir = /tmp/.ansible/cp
control_persist = 30s      # Keep SSH connection alive 30s

[defaults]
forks = 20                 # Run against 20 hosts in parallel (default: 5)

Execution Strategies

The default strategy is linear — all hosts complete each task before moving to the next.

# Free strategy: hosts advance independently
- name: Fast deployment
  hosts: webservers
  strategy: free
  tasks:
    ...

# Host-pinned: like free but pins to forks count
  strategy: host_pinned

Async Tasks

Use async for long-running tasks to avoid SSH timeouts:

- name: Run long database migration
  ansible.builtin.command: /opt/migrate.sh
  async: 3600    # Max runtime: 1 hour
  poll: 0        # Don't wait — fire and forget
  register: migration_job

- name: Wait for migration to complete
  ansible.builtin.async_status:
    jid: "{{ migration_job.ansible_job_id }}"
  register: job_result
  until: job_result.finished
  retries: 60
  delay: 30

Fact Caching

[defaults]
gathering = smart          # Only gather if no cached facts
fact_caching = redis
fact_caching_connection = localhost:6379
fact_caching_timeout = 86400  # Cache for 24 hours

Profiling with Callbacks

[defaults]
callbacks_enabled = ansible.posix.profile_tasks,ansible.posix.timer

# profile_tasks shows per-task timing:
# Tuesday 12 April 2024  11:23:45 +0000
# Install nginx ------- 4.32s
# Copy config --------- 1.11s

Limit Gathered Facts

# Gather only network facts
- hosts: all
  gather_facts: true
  gather_subset:
    - '!all'
    - network

Strategies Documentation →
